This is a greenish rock containing only 20% quartz, 35% albite, a mixture of chlorite and muscovite (40%) and 5% rutile.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss1.spectrum.txt

This is a coarse-grained sample composed of hornblende euhedra assocciated with biotite, with anhedra and subhedra of alkali feldspars (oligoclase and orthoclase) which are slightly sericitized. Zircon and pyrite crystals occur throughout the section. There may be a trace of quartz present as well. Some carbonate (?), or zircon masses (high relief, colorless, perhaps 3rd order B.R.) occur in this rock, often associated with the amphibole or opaques. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss7.spectrum.txt

This is a greenish, very fine-grained to microcrystalline rock containing somewhat larger recrystallized feldspar and carbonate grains. The finer material or groundmass also consists of these two phases in addition to a greenish microcrystalline mineral (chlorite?)Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.hornfels.coarse.hornfe2.spectrum.txt

This rock consists of a fine-grained to microcrystalline groundmass with a pleochroic brown phase (biotite?), opaques, cordierite and possibly some feldpsar. Large porphyroblasts of cordierite with heavy concentrations of tiny sillimanite needles are rimmed with cordierite and opaque granules.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.hornfels.coarse.hornfe3.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained rock containing anhedral and some euhedral dolomite. There are scattered anhedra and subhedra of monticellite, subhedral phlogopite and some rounded quartz grains present. These latter minerals often contain opaque inclusions, opaques (some at least of which are pyrite) are also disseminated throughout the entire sample. Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble1c.spectrum.txt

This sample consists of coarse-grained anhedral carbonate with numerous regions of serpentine (possibly brucite--MgOH2, at least in part) as an alteration/reaction of the carbonate. There are scattered subhedra of monticellite, tremolite, and phlogopite, with a few grains of idocrase (?) also noted in this section.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble2.spectrum.txt

A fine-grained rock with granulated dolomite and a trace of tremolite/actinolite, and possibly some phlogopite.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble4.spectrum.txt

A medium grained sample of anhedral calcite heavily altered to serpentine.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble5.spectrum.txt

This rock contains coarse-grained carbonate with scattered small subrounded quartz grains and minor subhedral tremolite which has altered to talc in a few spots.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble6.spectrum.txt

This sample consists of coarse-grained calcite poikiloblastically enclosing a finely disseminated opaque/ferric phase in the cores of the carbonate. There has been a considerable amount of granulation (cataclasis) with some carbonate grains showing bent lamellae. There are trace amounts of larger opaque phases in this rock.Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.coarse.marble7.spectrum.txt

This is a dark gray fine-grained to very fine-grained sample consisting of typical crenulated muscovite and quartz. Opaques, which usually have been rotated, occur throughout the sample -- pyrite, and either magnetite or hematite. There appear to be some carbonaceous granules scattered throughout the section as well. Modes were 65.9% muscovite, 33.5% quartz, 0.5% opaque and a trace of carbonaceous material. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.phyllite.coarse.phylli1.spectrum.txt

A schistose sample consisting of medium to coarse-grained recrystallized quartz with some muscovite that often contains ferruginous material. In addition there are scattered opaques present in this sample. The modal analyses for this rock are 88.2% quartz, 7.2% muscovite, 3.5% opaques and 1.1% other. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.coarse.qrtzit4.spectrum.txt

This medium to fine-grained rock contains aligned quartz grains showing strain, pressure solution effects, recrystallization and scattered fluid inclusions. Green bands or lenses contain cores of larger grains surrounded by smaller ones produced by mechanical breakage (cataclasis). These grains are colorless to very pale green and appear to be tremolite. Modes for this sample were 77.5% quartz and 22.5% amphibole. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.coarse.qrtzit2.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained quartzite composed of subrounded equant quartz with some secondary silica (chalcedony) and quartz deposition, sometimes the chalcedony is pseudomorphic to quartz grains. These quartz grains contain numerous fluid inclusions, are often strained and occasionally show pressure solution effects. Ferric material (plus clay?) is scattered throughout the sample giving this rock its reddish hue. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.coarse.qrtzit3.spectrum.txt

This rock contains coarse, irregular bands of brown, tan and gray material. Microscopically there are finer grains of calcite surrounded and enmeshed in a network of graphite bands and veins. There are also larger recrystallized calcite grains which are not associated with the graphite. Modes for this rock gave 62.5% calcite and 37.5% graphite. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist10.spectrum.txt

Coarse grains of euhedral to subhedral hornblende with lesser amounts of anhedral alkali feldspar characterize this sample. Modes were 78.4% hornblende and 21.6% feldspar. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist2.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained somewhat foliated rock. Bands of strained quartz with pressure solution effects alternate with irregular bands of biotite which have usually been altered to chlorite, associated with abundant magnetite and ferruginous staining. Much less muscovite, usually as sericite, is also present. Green to slightly blue-green epidote crystals occur commonly throughout the rock. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist3.spectrum.txt

This is a very coarse-grained rock containing euhedral brown tourmaline poikiloblastically enclosing quartz remnants, large subhedra of muscovite enclosing remnants of tourmaline or much lesser quartz, and recrystallized quartz. Magnetite occurs scattered throughout this rock as well. Modes were 35% quartz, 33% tourmaline, 30.6% muscovite and 1.4% opaques. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist4.spectrum.txt

This schistose rock contains abundant carbonaceous grains throughout the section along with quartz. The quartz grains are often strained, contain fluid inclusions, and evidence some pressure solution, while other regions of quartz have recrystallized. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist5.spectrum.txt

This is another coarse-grained rock composed of bent folia of tremolite and lenses and stringers of quartz. Unlike the previous one, however, this sample is completely unaltered. The bent folia are due to brittle deformation of the amphibole as a result of shear stress, with regions of concomittant granulation of this mineral. Modes for this rock gave 75.4% amphibole and 24.6% quartz. Particles size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist6.spectrum.txt
